channel.title: Noticias Forex — Global Investing
channel.description: Noticias forex de alto impacto en tiempo real con análisis fundamental para las 8 divisas G8.

item.title: Dollar Supported by Weak Stocks and Higher Bond Yields
item.description: The dollar index (DXY00 ) today is up by +0.38%. The dollar is moving higher today as the ongoing war with Iran drags on, boosting safe-haven demand for the dollar. Also, today's +3% jump in crude oil prices may stoke inflation and prompt the Fed to tighten monetary policy, a... Fuente: Nasdaq FX

item.title: US to deploy 3,000 82nd Airborne troops to Gulf amid Iran war
item.description: The US plans to deploy 3,000 82nd Airborne troops to the Gulf, boosting military readiness against Iran while keeping options open, as regional attacks intensify and ceasefire signals remain unclear. While we are all talking about the possibility of a ceasefire: Oil falls on report of possible one-month ceasefire under Witkoff-Kushner plan US-Iran ... Fuente: InvestingLive

item.title: Israel expands conflict footprint with strike on key Russia–Iran Caspian supply route.
item.description: Israel struck a major Russia–Iran supply route in the Caspian Sea, targeting Bandar Anzali port and highlighting expanding conflict scope and deepening Moscow–Tehran cooperation. The chatter is of a ceasefire, but there's a way to go until we get there: Oil falls on report of possible one-month ceasefire under Witkoff-Kushner plan US-Iran ceasefire... Fuente: InvestingLive

item.title: Dollar Advances on Reports the US to Deploy Troops to the Middle East
item.description: The dollar index (DXY00 ) on Tuesday rose by +0.42%. The dollar moved higher on Tuesday as the ongoing war with Iran drags on, boosting safe-haven demand for the dollar. Also, Tuesday's +4% jump in crude oil prices may stoke inflation and prompt the Fed to tighten monetary policy, a... Fuente: Nasdaq FX

item.title: Oil steady as US-Iran ceasefire talks face Israel uncertainty and broader Iran demands
item.description: Ceasefire speculation between the US and Iran has become more complex, with uncertainty over Israel’s support and Iran pushing for a broader agreement, even as a detailed 15-point proposal is reportedly already in play. Summary: Oil remains headline-driven as ceasefire speculation evolves. NYT reports uncertainty over whether Israel supports the pr... Fuente: InvestingLive

item.title: Financial & Forex Market Recap: March 24, 2026
item.description: Markets navigated conflicting US-Iran war narratives on Tuesday as equities clawed back early losses on peace-talk hopes, crude oil surged, and the dollar outperformed all major peers. Fuente: BabyPips

item.title: US-Iran ceasefire proposal is complex, 15 points need to be agreed. Hormuz would open.
item.description: Summary: Reports point to a proposed one-month ceasefire between the US and Iran. The framework is said to be led by Witkoff and Kushner. A 15-point agreement would be negotiated during the truce period. Terms focus heavily on dismantling Iran’s nuclear capabilities and curbing regional influence. Oil implications hinge on Hormuz remaining open and... Fuente: InvestingLive

item.title: investingLive Americas market news wrap: Waiting for real signs of war negotiations
item.description: Washington initiated outreach with Iran but nothing reached a level of negotiations - CNN US-led group waiting for response from Iran for peace talks on Thursday - report US Richmond Fed composite index 0 vs -10 expected US March S P Global flash services PMI 51.1 vs 51.5 expected SNB Chairman: We are prepared to introduce negative rates but the hu... Fuente: ForexLive

item.title: Australia CPI set to show sticky inflation in February, reinforcing hawkish RBA outlook
item.description: The Australian Bureau of Statistics (ABS) will release the Consumer Price Index (CPI) for February on Wednesday at 00:30 GMT, with inflation expected to hold steady at 3.8% YoY and come in flat on a monthly basis. Fuente: FXStreet

item.title: EUR/USD Price Forecast: RSI rebounds but bearish bias remains below 1.1600
item.description: The Euro (EUR) trades under pressure against the US Dollar (USD) on Tuesday, as heightened geopolitical risks surrounding the US-Israel war with Iran continue to underpin demand for the Greenback. Fuente: FXStreet

item.title: SNB Chairman: We are prepared to introduce negative rates but the hurdle is high
item.description: Policy rate is the main tool but there are situations where FX intervention is more suitable Negative interest rates worked in the past but they have side effects Interest rate differentials are an important driver of the exchange rate The Swiss franc hasn't moved on these headlines but the pair has recently bounced after hitting the lowest since 2... Fuente: ForexLive

item.title: Pound Sterling Price News and Forecast: GBP/USD slips as higher Oil, US yields boost the US Dollar
item.description: The Pound Sterling (GBP) falls by 0.16% on Tuesday as the US Dollar (USD) advances, underpinned by a recovery in energy prices and rising US Treasury yields, with markets not expecting the Federal Reserve (Fed) to cut rates in 2026. GBP/USD trades at 1.3400 after hitting a daily high of 1.3445. Fuente: FXStreet

item.title: U.S. Treasury to auction off $69 billion of 2-year notes at the top of the hour
item.description: The U.S. Treasury will auction off 2-year notes at the top of the hour. The auction is the 1st of 3 coupon auctions this week. Tomorrow the treasury will auction off of 5 year notes and on Thursday 7 year notes. Below are a review of the major components that the market will compare the actual results to the 6 month averages to determine strength o... Fuente: ForexLive

item.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Bears now target 0.6900
item.description: For the time being, the near-term outlook for the Australian Dollar (AUD) remains positive, supported by consistently strong inflation in Australia and the RBA's hawkish inclination. Fuente: FXStreet

item.title: GBP/USD slips as higher Oil, US yields boost the US Dollar
item.description: The Pound Sterling (GBP) falls by 0.16% on Tuesday as the US Dollar (USD) advances, underpinned by a recovery in energy prices and rising US Treasury yields, with markets not expecting the Federal Reserve (Fed) to cut rates in 2026. GBP/USD trades at 1.3400 after hitting a daily high of 1.3445. Fuente: FXStreet

item.title: tech falters while energy surges: today's market analysis
item.description: Sector Overview: Mixed Signals Across the Board Today's stock market snapshot reveals a mixed bag of performance across the sectors, with notable downturns in technology and resilience within the energy sector. The market's overall mood is one of caution, reflecting investors' uncertainty and varying confidence across industries. 📉 Technology Secto... Fuente: InvestingLive

item.title: Crude oil futures higher on the day but remains below target bullish bias levels
item.description: Crude oil futures saw a dramatic reversal yesterday, triggered by President Trump’s Truth Social comments, and the price action reflected just how fragile sentiment currently is in this market. The contract traded in an exceptionally wide range—from a high near $101.67 to a low around $84.37—a nearly $17 swing that underscores the heightened volati... Fuente: InvestingLive

item.title: EUR: ECB communication and PMIs shape outlook – TD Securities
item.description: TD Securities highlights the ECB Watchers’ Conference as a key platform for policymakers to discuss geopolitical risks and Eurozone monetary policy. The bank expects officials to reiterate that the ECB is ready to act but needs more time to assess risks. Fuente: FXStreet

item.title: BoE’s Pill: Ready to act if necessary
item.description: Bank of England’s (BoE) Chief Economist Huw Pill said that he stands ready to act against inflationary pressures stemming from the developments in the Middle East war in a text provided by the BoE on Tuesday. Fuente: FXStreet

item.title: BoE’s Pill: Uncertainty no excuse for inaction as inflation risks rise
item.description: BoE Chief Economist Huw Pill signaled growing concern over inflation risks stemming from the Middle East conflict, pointing to energy prices as the primary channel through which the shock will affect the UK economy. He described the current environment as one of “radical” uncertainty but stressed that commodity price movements will ultimately deter... Fuente: ActionForex

item.title: Bank of England's Pill: Stands ready to act against inflationary pressures
item.description: Ready to act against inflationary pressures stemming from developments in the Middle East to deliver price stability over the medium term Task of mon pol is to provide clarity on the pursuit of price stability Upside risks to price stability mounting due to the events in the gulf Current market pricing is for 69 bps of rate hikes this year with an ... Fuente: ForexLive

item.title: USDCAD holds support at key MA levels today, keeping the buyer in control.
item.description: The USDCAD has continued to show buying interest on dips, with the pair finding support against its 100-hour moving average in both the early Asian session and again during the early European session. That moving average — currently at 1.37258 — has become a key short-term barometer for buyers and sellers. So far, buyers are leaning against that le... Fuente: InvestingLive

item.title: Sterling Leads, Aussie Lags as PMIs Expose Uneven Stagflation Shock
item.description: Markets stayed cautious through the European session as a persistent “trust gap” around the Middle East conflict kept investors from fully embracing a peace pivot. Major European indexes traded sideways while US futures edged lower, reflecting a lack of conviction despite earlier optimism. Oil continues to anchor sentiment. Brent crude is holding i... Fuente: ActionForex

item.title: ECB's Sleijpen: Energy prices likely to become more-entrenched in economy than 2022
item.description: Comments from the Dutch central bank President: ECB very vigilant on possible second round effects ECB likely to have more info on second-round effects in April, but data will be limited More than any other central bank, the ECB has made a hawkish pivot in the wake of the energy price spike. That's awfully reminiscent of 2007 and Trichet's missteps... Fuente: ForexLive

item.title: Oro (XAU/USD) Hoy: Señales Técnicas Apuntan a un Fin de la Corrección - 24 marzo 2026
item.description: El oro muestra señales de rebote tras semanas de corrección. Atención a máximos y mínimos recientes, caída del crudo y dólar débil podrían impulsar XAU/USD. Fuente: DailyForex ES

item.title: UK PMIs show manufacturing cost inflation at highest since 1992 Sterling crisis
item.description: UK PMI data for March point to a sharp loss of growth momentum as the Middle East conflict begins to weigh on activity. PMI Manufacturing edged down from 51.7 to 51.4, while PMI Services dropped more notably from 53.9 to 51.2, a six-month low. As a result, PMI Composite declined from 53.7 to 51.0, also […] The post UK PMIs show manufacturing ... Fuente: ActionForex

item.title: Iran war makes second phase of LNG Canada more likely, TC Energy CEO says
item.description: The disruption to global liquefied natural gas supplies caused by the Iran war makes it more likely that a second stage of Canada's massive LNG Canada facility will be built, the CEO of Canadian pipeline company TC Energy predicted in Houston on Monday. While just weeks ago, market-watchers were talking about a potential glut of LNG globally, the I... Fuente: NewsData CAD

item.title: UK March flash services PMI 51.2 vs 53.0 expected
item.description: Prior 53.9 Manufacturing PMI 51.4 vs 50.1 expected Prior 51.7 Composite PMI 51.0 vs 52.9 expected Prior 53.7 Key Findings: Input price inflation jumps to its highest for just over three years amid a surge in manufacturing costs Comment: Chris Williamson, Chief Business Economist at S P Global Market Intelligence: “The war in the Middle East has hit... Fuente: ForexLive

item.title: IPO window closes again, yet private equity is still getting deals done
item.description: Initial public offerings have come to a virtual standstill since war in the Middle East broke out, but private equity is busy making deals elsewhere Fuente: NewsData CAD

item.title: Japan inflation slows but Mideast war could spark new rise
item.description: Japan's core inflation slowed to 1.6 percent in February, government data showed on Tuedsay, although it could soon rise again as the Middle East war sparks surges in oil prices. Fuente: NewsData EUR
